_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of computer programming principles such as object-oriented design.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, including data structures, control flow, object-oriented and functional programming patterns, standard library capabilities, and the data science ecosystem (NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn).
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-001 (OP, cosine: 0.465), DQ-K-014 (DQ, cosine: 0.462).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.601). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, object, oriented, programming]. DCWF-only terms [computer, design, principles] vs WIDAI-only terms [applications, capabilities, control, data, ecosystem, flow] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.892 (P=0.935, R=0.853). P exceeds R by 0.082 — the DCWF element's 9 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 30 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of computer programming principles such as object-oriented design." and "Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, inc" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.475 < STS 0.601 (gap -0.126). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [computer, design, principles] and [applications, capabilities, control] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.2%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, object, oriented, programming]. DCWF-only: [computer, design, principles]. WIDAI-only: [applications, capabilities, control, data, ecosystem, flow].
